title: Italians have it easy with Rebuild
display_title: Italians have it easy with Rebuild
wikitext:
[]: Italians have it easy with Rebuild             Quote Post by xdiesp » Fri Dec 17, 2010 5:00 am Over here the italian state tv is going to broadcast Eva 1.11 and 2.22 for a Christmas special. But it will be dubbed and after fifteen years I can't listen to anything but the original voices. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Fri Dec 17, 2010 11:51 am just rub it in the american faces. greetings from germany, we got our rebuild releases as well but i shudder at the thought of it being broadcasted on tv. it would be just unbearably cut up.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Fri Dec 17, 2010 12:43 pm That's great! They will be broadcasted by Rai 4 on Thursday 23 and 30. Since they will be airing around 11pm I guess they'll be pretty much uncut. I usually prefer subs, but it's interesting none the less, I haven't had the chance to listen to the dub of 2.22 yet. Damn. They're going to air Akira on Christmas Day at 2pm! I wonder just how much they're going to cut... More info (in Italian, of course) on Dynit's site .

[]:             Quote Post by ShadowGT » Fri Dec 17, 2010 10:59 pm Is the Dub in English or Italian? I'm assuming the latter.

[]:             Quote Post by Hatsumi92 » Sat Dec 18, 2010 12:04 am But, Evangelion 1.11 has been on Funimation "On-Demand" (for a price iirc) several times.

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Sat Dec 18, 2010 1:37 am Failed to mention this channel can be watched online, will provide the link when the show's up. ShadowGT wrote: Is the Dub in English or Italian? I'm assuming the latter.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sat Dec 18, 2010 4:09 am Aww, I won't go back home for Christmas unfortunately... but I'm happy for you guys. I wonder if the streaming version will be accessible from outside Italy... (I'm curious about the dub).

[]:             Quote Post by Tetsuo87 » Sat Dec 18, 2010 4:37 pm Rai4 is truly a great broadcaster, however, here you can find the promo: [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hjh9S7bQqjE[/url] (there are also moments with the Italian dubbing) Rai4 also be seen in free streaming for residents in Italy, if you want to see from outside Italy have to use a proxy (as is done for the Japanese trailer of eva) you can find live stream at Rai.tv (this link: [url]http://www.rai.tv/dl/RaiTV/diretta.html?cid=PublishingBlock-ea74b386-9a37-4293-a06b-c003359c6ede&channel=Rai%204[/url])

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sat Dec 18, 2010 6:31 pm Something like this would never happen on mainstream UK TV. The best bet would be channel 4 - i think theyve shown Ghibli stuff in the past. Wiki tells me that NHE was shown on the Sci-Fi channel back in 02 but thats a relatively obscure cable channel.

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Sat Dec 18, 2010 9:50 pm Thank you for the info, Tetsuo .

[]:             Quote Post by lo zaffo » Mon Dec 20, 2010 2:31 pm I'll manage to see how rai4's DVB-T broadcast will look good. I have both DVDs, Dynit Italian version, to compare with.

[]:             Quote Post by Tetsuo87 » Mon Dec 20, 2010 3:08 pm LiLi wrote: Thank you for the info, Tetsuo .

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Thu Dec 23, 2010 3:45 pm Eva 1.11 in 1 hour, but I'm not sure it will be accessible from every (if any) state.

[]:             Quote Post by Tetsuo87 » Thu Dec 23, 2010 6:02 pm its now on air, very good quality, the bitrate is around 6Mbps, here some screenshot [URL=http://img715.imageshack.us/i/capture20101223225908.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://img713.imageshack.us/i/capture20101223234537.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://img828.imageshack.us/i/capture20101223234544.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://img193.imageshack.us/i/capture20101223234547.jpg/] [/URL][URL=http://img577.imageshack.us/i/capture20101223234551.jpg/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Eva 04 » Thu Dec 23, 2010 8:37 pm xdiesp wrote: Eva 1.11 in 1 hour, but I'm not sure it will be accessible from every (if any) state.

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Thu Dec 23, 2010 9:28 pm just rub it in the american faces.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Thu Dec 23, 2010 9:50 pm gigantor was pretty popular in the u.s. too, though that's been awhile. yeah, i know this shows are bigger in europe. i was just kidding because of the title. usually it's america who gets all the cool shit first.

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Fri Dec 24, 2010 4:37 am Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion wrote: eventually find their way to Italy long before they do in the US

[]:             Quote Post by distopia » Sun Dec 26, 2010 4:08 pm Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion wrote: That's not it, it's the fact that most mecha anime, mainly super robot ones, eventually find their way to Italy long before they do in the US (provided A. they actually get to the US and B. don't get horrible and sloppy dub jobs). I don't know the history 100%, but I am aware that there many more old super robot anime got aired there when very few ever got to the US, the only one of them to gain serious popularity was Golion (redubbed here in the US as Voltron).

[]:             Quote Post by xdiesp » Thu Dec 30, 2010 1:16 pm Eva 2.22 is tonight, same channel. In about... 4 hours.
